{"society_power": 8.5, "state_power": 7.5, "society_change": 0.0, "state_change": 0.0, "key_event": "Decentralization and rights reforms offset by nationalization-privatization wash", "reasoning": "The nationalizations (1981) and their reversal via privatization (1986) largely cancel out on state economic power, while decentralization and abolition of the death penalty modestly constrain the state but are balanced by its continued strong administrative capacity, keeping state power roughly steady. Society's class-based labor pillar eroded but was substantially replaced by pluralistic new social movements that repeatedly forced government retreats (Savary, Devaquet), so society power holds firm at its high level."}
